title: China-to-Ghana Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: GSA Product Certification and Import Conformity for Solar PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: The Ghana Standards Authority (GSA) is mandated under the Ghana Standards Authority Act, 2022 (Act 1078) to require conformity assessment for imported electrical and energy equipment. Solar PV inverters fall within the category of solar energy appliances that GSA lists as subject to product certification testing. Imported electrical goods classified as high-risk goods (HRG) require GSA inspection and a Certificate of Conformity (CoC) for customs clearance. Exporters should confirm whether a pre-shipment conformity inspection or a GSA-recognised product certificate route applies to their specific inverter HS code and shipment.
target_standards: GSA Product Certification Scheme — solar energy appliances scope
Certificate of Conformity (CoC) for high-risk goods at Ghana customs
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese exporters commonly hold GB/T 37408-2019 (technical requirements for PV inverters) and CQC or CCC marks for the domestic market. These Chinese certificates do not substitute for Ghana GSA conformity documents, but the underlying test reports (IEC 62109-based) may support a Ghana conformity submission where an accredited lab issued them.
rows.gap: The exporter should confirm the Ghana HS code for their inverter model, engage a GSA-accredited or approved conformity assessment body, and obtain a Certificate of Conformity before shipment. Chinese domestic certificates cannot substitute for the CoC and should not be presented as Ghana clearance evidence without explicit acceptance by the GSA or approved body.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for solar energy appliances classified as high-risk goods imported into Ghana; CoC required for customs clearance
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 37408-2019 is a recommended national standard; CQC is voluntary; CCC mandatory scope does not include standard grid-tied PV inverters

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GSA conformity assessment and a Certificate of Conformity are required for solar PV inverter imports into Ghana. Chinese domestic certificates do not substitute; engage a GSA-approved body before shipment.

rows.requirement: Energy Commission LI 2461 Inverter Standards and Labelling Regulations (2022)
rows.target_requirement: The Renewable Energy (Standards and Labelling) (Inverters) Regulations, 2022 (LI 2461), made under the Energy Commission Act 1997 (Act 541), establish minimum performance and labelling requirements for inverters placed on the Ghanaian market. The regulations mandate that inverters meet specified efficiency and technical parameters and carry required labels to assist consumers and enforcement officers in identifying compliant products. Compliance with LI 2461 is required for market access and may intersect with GSA product certification for inverter equipment.
target_standards: Renewable Energy (Standards and Labelling) (Inverters) Regulations, 2022 — LI 2461
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese inverters may carry GB/T 37408-2019 performance ratings and Chinese energy efficiency labels. These do not automatically satisfy LI 2461 labelling and performance requirements for the Ghanaian market. The exporter should verify whether their product's rated efficiency and technical data meet the LI 2461 parameters and arrange compliant labelling for Ghana.
china_standards: China energy efficiency labelling scheme (MEPS label)
rows.gap: Chinese efficiency labels and GB/T performance documentation are not substitutes for LI 2461 compliance. The exporter should obtain the LI 2461 text from the Energy Commission, confirm the applicable efficiency tiers and technical parameters for their inverter type, and arrange Ghana-compliant labelling before shipment.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for inverters placed on the Ghanaian market under LI 2461; Energy Commission-administered
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 37408-2019 is a recommended standard; Chinese energy efficiency labels are not required for export

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] LI 2461 inverter standards and labelling requirements are mandatory for the Ghanaian market. Chinese performance labels do not substitute; confirm LI 2461 parameters and arrange compliant Ghana labelling before shipment.

source.publisher: Ghana Energy Commission — Standards and Labelling Programme

rows.requirement: IEC 62109 Safety Requirements for PV Inverters — Ghana Market Expectation
rows.target_requirement: IEC 62109-1 (general safety requirements) and IEC 62109-2 (specific requirements for inverters) are the internationally recognised safety standards for solar PV inverters. Ghana's GSA, as a member of the IEC, references international standards in its conformity assessment activities, and IEC 62109 is the benchmark safety standard expected by the Ghanaian market and GSA-approved conformity assessment bodies for PV inverters. Test reports from IECEE CB Scheme-accredited laboratories based on IEC 62109-1/-2 are likely to support Ghana conformity submissions.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1:2010 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2:2011 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverters are commonly certified to GB/T 37408-2019 and may hold IECEE CB reports based on IEC 62109. Where the Chinese manufacturer holds an IECEE CB report from an accredited laboratory, this is the most useful supporting document for Ghana conformity assessment. A GB/T 37408 report alone is not equivalent but the underlying test data may be leveraged.
china_standards: IECEE CB report based on IEC 62109-1/-2 (if held)
rows.gap: Manufacturers should obtain or verify existing IECEE CB test reports based on IEC 62109-1/-2 from an accredited lab. Where only a GB/T 37408 report exists, assess whether it was conducted by an IECEE-accredited laboratory and whether the test scope maps to IEC 62109 requirements, or commission supplemental testing.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as the applicable international safety standard referenced by GSA for PV inverter conformity assessment in Ghana
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 37408-2019 is a recommended standard; IEC 62109 adoption is partial in Chinese national standards

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] IEC 62109-1/-2 is the benchmark safety standard for PV inverters in Ghana. An IECEE CB report from an accredited lab is the most portable supporting document; a GB/T 37408-only dossier is not equivalent.

rows.requirement: ECG / NEDCo Grid Connection and Net Metering Code 2023 — 50 Hz, Anti-Islanding, Power Factor
rows.target_requirement: Ghana operates on a 50 Hz, 230 V (single-phase) / 400 V (three-phase) grid. The Energy Commission Net Metering Code 2023 (made under Act 832) governs grid-connected renewable energy generating systems feeding into the ECG and NEDCo distribution networks. Key technical requirements include: inverter operation at 50 Hz; anti-islanding protection; power factor ≥ 0.98 at rated output; single-phase inverters up to 13.8 kVA; three-phase or special consideration above 13.8 kVA; smart net meter installation; compliance with the Net Metering Sub-Code technical annex; and registration with the Energy Commission before connection.
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connected PV inverters are designed for 50 Hz and commonly reference GB/T 19964-2012 (technical requirements for PV power station access to power systems) and GB/T 37408 for grid interaction. Anti-islanding is addressed in GB/T 37408, but the specific power factor threshold (≥ 0.98), capacity thresholds, and registration requirements in Ghana differ from Chinese domestic grid codes and must be verified against the Net Metering Code 2023.
rows.gap: Exporters should confirm that inverters are factory-configured for 50 Hz operation and anti-islanding protection meeting the Net Metering Code 2023 parameters. Power factor capability ≥ 0.98 should be verified in the technical datasheet. Energy Commission registration of the installation is required before ECG/NEDCo connection. Installers in Ghana must follow the net metering application portal process.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected installations; Energy Commission registration required before ECG/NEDCo connection; Net Metering Code 2023 applies
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 19964-2012 and GB/T 37408 anti-islanding requirements are mandatory for grid-connected systems in China

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Ghana's 50 Hz grid and Net Metering Code 2023 impose specific anti-islanding, power factor, and capacity requirements that must be confirmed in the inverter datasheet and factory configuration before export. Energy Commission registration is mandatory before ECG/NEDCo connection.
